title: Docker: Containers vs VMs — Why Docker Matters
content:
### Day 181: Docker: Containers vs VMs — Why Docker Matters

**Containers vs VMs:**
- **VM:** Full OS copy — heavy, slow to start
- **Container:** Shares host OS kernel — lightweight, instant start

**Docker:** Package app + dependencies into a portable container.
- Runs same everywhere: dev, staging, production
codeExample:
# Day 181: Docker: Containers vs VMs — Why Docker Matters
# Docker concepts
print("=== Containers vs VMs ===")
print("VM:        [App] [Libs] [Guest OS] [Hypervisor] [Hardware]")
print("Container: [App] [Libs] [Docker Engine] [Host OS] [Hardware]")
print()
print("Docker commands:")
print("  docker pull python:3.10     # Download image")
print("  docker run -it python:3.10  # Run interactive")
print("  docker ps                   # List running")
print("  docker images               # List images")
print("  docker stop <id>            # Stop container")
print("  docker rm <id>              # Remove container")
keyTakeaways: VM: Full OS copy — heavy, slow to start
Container: Shares host OS kernel — lightweight, instant start
Runs same everywhere: dev, staging, production
